examples.title: Phong Illumination Calculation
examples.description: Computes Phong lighting model for a single light source
examples.explanation: The Phong model combines three light components: ambient for base illumination, diffuse for matte surfaces following Lambert's law, and specular for shiny highlights. The reflection vector is computed using the built-in reflect function.

examples.title: Point Light Attenuation
examples.description: Calculates light intensity attenuation based on distance
examples.explanation: Attenuation simulates real-world light falloff. Quadratic term (c3*d²) dominates at long distances, creating realistic light falloff. Typical values: c1=1, c2=0, c3=1 for physically accurate inverse-square falloff.

examples.title: Gouraud Shading Implementation
examples.code:
// Gouraud Shading - Vertex-based

STRUCTURE Vertex:
 position: vec3
 normal: vec3
 color: vec3
END STRUCTURE

STRUCTURE Polygon:
 vertices: array[3] of Vertex
END STRUCTURE

FUNCTION GouraudShade(polygon, light, material):
 // Step 1: Calculate lighting at each vertex
 FOR EACH vertex IN polygon.vertices:
 vertex.color = PhongIllumination(
 vertex.normal,
 light.direction,
 camera.position, // View direction
 material.ka, material.kd, material.ks,
 material.shininess,
 light.ambient, light.diffuse
 )
 
 // Step 2: Interpolate across polygon for each pixel
 // (Done by rasterizer using barycentric coordinates)
 FOR EACH pixel ON polygon:
 // Linear interpolation of vertex colors
 pixel.color = interpolate(polygon.vertices[0].color,
 polygon.vertices[1].color,
 polygon.vertices[2].color,
 barycentric_coords)
 
 RETURN pixel_colors
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Gouraud shading computes lighting at vertices during vertex processing, then the rasterizer interpolates these color values across the polygon. Efficient but may miss specular highlights within polygons.

examples.title: Spotlight Intensity Calculation
examples.description: Calculates spotlight contribution based on cone angle and falloff
examples.explanation: The spotlight calculation determines if a surface point falls within the light cone. The exponent controls how quickly light fades from center (high = sharp edge) to outside (low = soft edge).

examples.title: Blinn-Phong Lighting
examples.description: More efficient specular calculation using halfway vector
examples.explanation: Blinn-Phong replaces reflection vector computation with halfway vector (H), which is computationally cheaper. The H vector is always in the upper hemisphere avoiding sign issues. Standard in modern GPU pipelines.
